items.prompt: Which practice helps prevent silent overwrites when a feature matrix evolves?
items.explain: Table 1.8 lists data version control to track versions and avoid overwriting important changes.

items.prompt: Privacy and security controls are optional once a churn model reaches production.
items.explain: The chapter states management and protection begin when records are stored and continue through reuse—not afterthoughts.

items.prompt: Which control reduces re-identification risk when sharing research extracts?
choices: Publishing raw national IDs by default
items.explain: Table 1.9 lists anonymization and de-identification among privacy practices for shared extracts.

items.prompt: Name one reason traceability matters in dataset management.
items.rubric: Accept audits, explaining how a figure was produced, collaboration, or reproducibility.
items.sample_answer: Traceability lets teams show how a reported figure was produced when auditors or collaborators ask.
